notes:
Albany has no existing page/baseline in this repo yet (it's a 'Phase 2' city per pickleheads-court-research-prompts.md — researched for data quality, not yet built a cities/albany.html page). Pickleheads' Albany city index (pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/albany) lists exactly 2 locations totaling 10 courts: Ocean View Park (4 outdoor) and Albany High School (6 indoor). Both are recorded below with matchStatus 'new' / existingId null (omitted from each venue object per task instructions, since there's nothing to reconcile against).

PRIMARY LEAD, RESOLVED: this repo's pickleheads-research/merge-report.md flagged a venue at 900 Buchanan St as 'LIKELY MISCATEGORIZED BY PICKLEHEADS,' since Pickleheads lists the same venue under BOTH the berkeley slug (pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/berkeley/ocean-view-park, recorded in this repo as pickleheads-research/berkeley.json's 'ocean-view-park' entry, not yet merged into assets/courts-data.json) and the albany slug (used here). That venue is Ocean View Park, recorded below. Albany's own official city website settles the question unambiguously: 900 Buchanan St is an Albany city park (jointly owned by the City of Albany and the Albany Unified School District), administered by Albany's own Recreation & Community Services Department (1249 Marin Ave, Albany), with fees set on Albany's own municipal fee schedule, and it's the exact location Albany's own Adult-Sports/Pickleball page names for the city's Tuesday pickleball open play. This is not a Berkeley venue — it just sits close to the Berkeley border (Buchanan St runs along that border), which is almost certainly why Pickleheads double-listed it. A human should retire/delete the berkeley.json 'ocean-view-park' duplicate rather than keep both when merging, since both files currently use the identical id 'ocean-view-park' and would collide in assets/courts-data.json as-is.

False positives ruled out from the common city name 'Albany' (none of these are Albany, CA / Alameda County, so none are included below): Albany JCC (albanyjcc.org — actually Albany, NY, 340 Whitehall Rd), Albany Tennis Club (albanytennisclub.org — actually Albany, OR), the 'Albany Area YMCA' listed on Pickleheads (actually Albany, GA — pickleheads.com/courts/us/georgia/albany/albany-area-ymca), and 'ACHF Pickleball Courts' / 'ACHF Pickleball Court 2' on albanycounty.recdesk.com (actually Albany County, NY).

Other real Albany, CA facilities investigated and excluded: Memorial Park (1325 Portland Ave) and Jewel's Terrace Park (1548 Terrace St) both appear on Albany's own citywide 'Tennis & Pickleball Courts' page alongside Ocean View Park, but independent court databases (globaltennisnetwork.com for Memorial Park, tennispickleballcalifornia.com for Jewel's Terrace) both describe them as tennis-only with 0 pickleball courts / no pickleball striping — excluded as not actually pickleball venues. Albany YMCA (921 Kains Ave, a YMCA of the East Bay branch) was also investigated: its own site (ymcaeastbay.org/health-and-fitness/pickleball) states only that it 'offers semi-regular pickleball learning clinics for beginners' and explicitly tells Albany members to 'play at our other locations' for actual open play — unlike the Berkeley/Oakland/Pleasant Hill/Richmond Y branches described on that same page, no court count is given for Albany and there's no standing open-play schedule. That reads as thin/intermittent clinic use of shared gym space rather than a confirmed standing pickleball venue, so — per this site's no-padding standard — it was left out entirely rather than included with a guessed court count. A human could add it later if the Y publishes specifics.

Weather line for both venues below is a fresh assessment written for Albany specifically (not copied from Berkeley, since Albany has no existing courts-data.json entries to copy from): Albany's outdoor court site sits on the flat bayside corridor directly across the bay from the Golden Gate gap, immediately north of west Berkeley's flatland courts, so it should share Berkeley's pattern of afternoon marine wind and summer fog — if anything slightly breezier, given the open shoreline near the Albany Bulb just south of Ocean View Park.

venues.name: Ocean View Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court — dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.price: Free walk-up (first-come-first-served); reservations $11/hr resident, $13/hr non-resident per court, bookable in 30-minute blocks — per Albany's official Tennis & Pickleball Courts fee page, fetched live for this research. Several third-party aggregators instead cite $10.37/$12.44 or $9.50/$11.50 per hour resident/non-resident — likely stale copies of a prior fiscal year's fee schedule; the live city page is treated as authoritative.
venues.hours: Court hours 8am-10pm daily per the city's Tennis & Pickleball Courts fee page; park gate hours are separately listed as 7am-9pm (Apr 1-Oct 31) / 7am-8pm (Nov 1-Mar 31) on the park's own page — the two published ranges don't fully nest and this wasn't resolved further. Dedicated 'Outdoor Pickleball Open Play' (player-led, no instructor) runs Tuesdays 4-6pm.
venues.weather: Flat bayside flatland directly across the bay from the Golden Gate gap, immediately north of west Berkeley's flatland courts — expect the same pattern of afternoon marine wind and summer fog as Berkeley, if anything a touch breezier given the open shoreline near the Albany Bulb just south of the park.
venues.notes:
THE venue behind this file's primary research lead — see top-level notes for the full Berkeley/Albany miscategorization writeup. Court count (4) independently corroborated by Pickleheads' own venue page ('4 outdoor hard courts... dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets'), globalpickleball.network (both its Albany city listing and its dedicated Ocean View Park court page), and this repo's own prior berkeley.json research (which separately cited countyoffice.org and bounce.game, both also saying 4). One outlier, tennispickleballcalifornia.com, states 6 courts — almost certainly confusion with Albany High School's 6 indoor courts less than a mile away. A second, minor, unresolved conflict: Pickleheads describes 'permanent... nets' while globalpickleball.network's listing tags this venue 'Shared Nets' instead of 'Dedicated' — noted but not treated as blocking 'confirmed' since it doesn't affect the core court-count/outdoor facts, and every other source (including the physical description on Albany's own park page) is consistent with genuinely dedicated pickleball courts separate from the park's one tennis court.

Real, ongoing community activity corroborates current operation (not just a stale listing): BORP (Bay Area Outreach & Recreation Program) runs a weekly Adaptive Pickleball program here — Thursdays 5-6:30pm and Saturdays 1-4pm per borp.org, open to players/family/volunteers of any age or skill level, wheelchair users welcome, players with disabilities get an extra bounce. Pickleheads' own page shows 198 registered players and 3 groups active at this court. A Google Maps Local Guide review specifically calls out 'Great Pickleball Ball courts with good nighttime lighting' (independently confirms the lights amenity), and — fittingly for this record — another reviewer's aside ('this is the best part of Berkeley even though it isn't officially Berkeley') is a nice illustration of exactly the confusion this record resolves.

lat/lon and googleMapsUrl are a verified numeric cid, not a guess: navigated to the Google Maps place page for 'Ocean View Park' near Albany, extracted the hex feature id from the resulting URL, converted the second hex component to decimal, and round-tripped maps.google.com/?cid=3168069807741376823 to confirm it resolves back to this exact park (900 Buchanan St, Albany, CA 94706; 4.4 stars, 218 reviews — a general Google rating, not a Pickleheads rating, so not used for pickleheadsRating, which Pickleheads doesn't clearly expose as a star value on this venue's page and is left null). bookingUrl is Albany Rec & Community Services' WebTrac reservation portal, linked from the 'Register Here' button on the city's own Tennis & Pickleball Courts page (used the stable splash-page URL rather than the session-specific csrf-token URL the button actually generates, since that token expires).

venues.name: Albany High School
venues.surface: Indoor wood court (school gymnasium floor); permanent lines painted, no fixed net
venues.netType: BYO net (permanent lines only; bring your own portable net)
venues.price: Fee required — Pickleheads states 'a one-time fee is required to play' but no dollar amount; a stale 2020 third-party community listing (Places2Play) separately cited $5 entry + $2 paddle rental for a Sunday drop-in run at this address, not corroborated by any current source. See notes.
venues.hours: Not specified by any current source. A 2020 third-party listing (Places2Play) cited Sundays 4-7pm for a now-possibly-defunct drop-in — see notes; not treated as current.
venues.notes: Included because Pickleheads lists it as one of Albany's 2 pickleball locations — 6 indoor wood courts, permanent lines, BYO net, one-time fee, restrooms — consistently between Pickleheads' city index and its own venue detail page. Set confirmed: false because current OPERATIONAL STATUS (does this actually run right now?) is genuinely unresolved, even though the physical facts (6 indoor wood courts, BYO net, fee, restrooms) are consistent everywhere this venue is mentioned: (1) Albany's own official Adult-Sports/Pickleball page (fetched live for this research, July 2026) does not mention Albany High School at all — it names only Ocean View Park for the city's pickleball offering; (2) globalpickleball.network's Albany listing (which aggregates a wide radius across many East Bay cities and still finds room for e.g. Berkeley/El Cerrito/Oakland venues) also does not include Albany High School, only Ocean View Park; (3) the one source with an explicit operational-status claim, Places2Play (places2play.org/place?id=456), states in capitals 'DUE TO MAINTENANCE ISSUES IN THE GYM, THIS SITE IS CURRENTLY UNAVAILABLE FOR PICKLEBALL USE' — but that page is dated Sunday, September 27, 2020, nearly six years before this research, so it's unclear whether that closure ever lifted, became permanent, or the listing was simply abandoned rather than updated. Places2Play's description of the pre-closure program (Sundays 4-7pm, $5 entry + $2 paddle rental, a community contact named 'Bell,' entrance on the baseball-field side) also reads like an informally-run community drop-in rather than a city-administered one, which would explain its absence from Albany's own Recreation Dept pages either way. Notably, Pickleheads' Ocean View Park listing separately credits 'Source: Bella' — possibly the same community organizer behind both original listings, which, if true, would mean neither Pickleheads listing has necessarily been independently refreshed since. Net assessment: no source contradicts the court count or indoor/outdoor facts, but nothing current corroborates that this venue is still operating, so this record is conservatively marked unconfirmed rather than assumed active. A human should call Albany Recreation & Community Services (510-524-9283) or Albany High School (510-558-2500) to verify status before relying on this listing. bookingUrl left null — no reservation portal found, and Pickleheads' own page shows a 'Schedule' option but no 'Reserve' button for this venue (unlike Ocean View Park, which has both), consistent with a pay-at-the-door model. lights: true follows this repo's existing convention of treating staffed/enclosed indoor gymnasiums as inherently lit even when no source states it explicitly (see e.g. san-mateo-high-school-gym in pickleheads-research/san-mateo.json for the same reasoning). googleMapsUrl cid verified by the same round-trip method as Ocean View Park: resolved 'Albany High School' near Albany, CA on Google Maps (address and phone matched ahs.ausdk12.org exactly), converted its hex feature id to decimal, and confirmed maps.google.com/?cid=16268999382975675321 resolves back to the same school.
